properties.description: This policy initiative defines the security requirements for Virtual Machines

metadata.displayName: VM-001 Effect: Network interfaces should disable IP forwarding
metadata.description: 'Audit' allows a non-compliant resource to be created, but flags it as non-compliant. 'Deny' blocks the resource creation. 'Disable' turns off the policy.

metadata.displayName: VM-002 Effect: Network interfaces should not have public IPs
metadata.description: 'Audit' allows a non-compliant resource to be created, but flags it as non-compliant. 'Deny' blocks the resource creation. 'Disable' turns off the policy.

metadata.displayName: VM-003 Effect: Machines should be configured to periodically check for missing system updates
metadata.description: 'Audit' allows a non-compliant resource to be created, but flags it as non-compliant. 'Deny' blocks the resource creation. 'Disable' turns off the policy.

metadata.displayName: VM-004 Effect: Adaptive application controls for defining safe applications should be enabled on your machines
metadata.description: Enable or disable the execution of the policy

metadata.displayName: VM-005 Effect: Internet-facing virtual machines should be protected with network security groups
metadata.description: Enable or disable the execution of the policy

metadata.displayName: VM-006 Effect: All network ports should be restricted on network security groups associated to your virtual machine
metadata.description: Enable or disable the execution of the policy

metadata.displayName: VM-007 Effect: Only approved VM extensions should be installed
metadata.description: Enable or disable the execution of the policy
VM-007: The list of approved extension types that can be installed. Example: AzureDiskEncryption
metadata.displayName: Approved VM extensions
VM-008 Effect: RDP and SSH access from the Internet should be blocked
metadata.description: Enable or disable the execution of the policy

metadata.displayName: VM-009 Effect: Deploy default Microsoft IaaSAntimalware extension for Windows Server with custom configurations
metadata.description: Enable or disable the execution of the policy

metadata.displayName: VM-009: Evaluation Delay for anti-malware Extension policy
metadata.description: Specifies when the existence of the related resources should be evaluated. The delay is only used for evaluations that are a result of a create or update resource request. Allowed values are AfterProvisioning, AfterProvisioningSuccess, AfterProvisioningFailure, or an ISO 8601 duration between 0 and 360 minutes.

metadata.displayName: VM-009: Antimalware VM Extension Enable Real-Time Protection
metadata.description: Indicates whether or not real time protection is enabled (default is true)

metadata.displayName: VM-009: Antimalware VM Extension Excluded files and locations
metadata.description: Semicolon delimited list of file paths or locations to exclude from scanning. We recommend avoiding the use of wildcard characters. For example: c:\excluded-path-1;c:\excluded-path-2

metadata.displayName: VM-009: Antimalware VM Extension Excluded files extensions
metadata.description: Semicolon delimited list of file extensions to exclude from scanning. We recommend avoiding the use of wildcard characters. For example: .ext1;.ext2

metadata.displayName: VM-009: Antimalware VM Extension Excluded processes
metadata.description: Semicolon delimited list of process names to exclude from scanning. No wildcard characters are permitted. For example: excludedproc1.exe;excludedproc2.exe

metadata.displayName: VM-009: Antimalware VM Extension Run a scheduled scan
metadata.description: Indicates whether or not custom scheduled scan settings are enabled (default is false)

metadata.displayName: VM-009: Antimalware VM Extension Scan type
metadata.description: Indicates whether scheduled scan setting type is set to Quick or Full (default is Quick when scheduled scan is enabled)

metadata.displayName: VM-009: Antimalware VM Extension Scan day
metadata.description: Day of the week for scheduled scan (1-Sunday, 2-Monday, ..., 7-Saturday)

metadata.displayName: VM-009: Antimalware VM Extension Scan time
metadata.description: When to perform the scheduled scan, measured in minutes from midnight (0-1440). For example: 0 = 12AM, 60 = 1AM, 120 = 2AM.

metadata.displayName: VM-009: Antimalware VM ExtensionOptional: List of VM images that have supported Windows OS to add to scope for Antimalware VM Extension policy
metadata.description: Example values: '/subscriptions/<subscriptionId>/resourceGroups/YourResourceGroup/providers/Microsoft.Compute/images/ContosoStdImage'

metadata.displayName: VM-009: Optional: List of VM Publishers that have supported Windows OS to add to scope for Antimalware VM Extension policy
metadata.description: Example values: 'center-for-internet-security-inc'

metadata.displayName: VM-009: Optional: List of VM Offers that have supported Windows OS to add to scope for Antimalware VM Extension policy
metadata.description: Example values: 'cis-windows-server-2019-v1-0-0-l1'

metadata.displayName: VM-009: Optional: List of VM SKU that have supported Windows OS to add to scope for Antimalware VM Extension policy
metadata.description: Example values: 'cis-ws2019-l1'

metadata.displayName: VM-010 Effect: Deploy Virtual Machine Auto Shutdown Schedule
metadata.description: Enable or disable the execution of the policy

metadata.displayName: VM-010: Evaluation Delay for VM Shutdown Schedule policy
metadata.description: Specifies when the existence of the related resources should be evaluated. The delay is only used for evaluations that are a result of a create or update resource request. Allowed values are AfterProvisioning, AfterProvisioningSuccess, AfterProvisioningFailure, or an ISO 8601 duration between 0 and 360 minutes.

metadata.displayName: VM-010: VM Shutdown Schedule time
metadata.description: Specifies the time for VM Shutdown schedule.

metadata.displayName: VM-010: VM Shutdown Schedule time zone Id
metadata.description: Specifies the timezone Id for the VM Shutdown schedule policy.

metadata.displayName: VM-010: Send Notification before auto-shutdown for VM Shutdown schedule
metadata.description: If notifications are enabled for this schedule (i.e. Enabled, Disabled).

metadata.displayName: VM-010: Email Address for VM Shutdown schedule notification
metadata.description: Email address to be used for notification

metadata.displayName: VM-010: Webhook URL for VM Shutdown schedule notification
metadata.description: A notification will be posted to the specified webhook endpoint when the auto-shutdown is about to happen.
